title: Notebook Wars — Client Anomalies (Full Reporting)
description: Anomaly events proactively reported by clients across all players (FEATURE_FLAGS_DESIGN §9.7). Unlike targeted collection: not constrained by allowPublicIds — any client that encounters memory over threshold (mem) / sustained CPU saturation (cpu) / WebGL context lost (webgl_lost) / main loop freeze (anr) / uncaught exception (jserror) / last session crash (crash) reports directly. Labels are only {source=client, kind=anomaly}; type/publicId/platform/detail/msg are inlined (logfmt).

list.name: publicId
list.label: Player publicId (9 digits, leave blank = all)

panels.title: Anomaly Event Rate (by type)
panels.description: Rate of kind=anomaly events within the time window, stacked by type. No data = no client anomalies reported network-wide (or metaserver NW_LOKI_PUSH_URL not configured).

targets.expr: sum by (type) (count_over_time({source="client", kind="anomaly"} | logfmt | type=~"$type" | platform=~"$platform" | publicId=~"$publicId.*" |~ "(?i)$search" [$__auto]))

panels.title: Crash Count (crash, within window)
panels.description: Number of crash events reported on next startup after the previous session exited abnormally (OOM / process killed / tab killed).

targets.expr: sum(count_over_time({source="client", kind="anomaly"} | logfmt | type="crash" | platform=~"$platform" | publicId=~"$publicId.*" [$__auto]))
sum(count_over_time({source="client", kind="anomaly"} | logfmt | type=~"$type" | platform=~"$platform" | publicId=~"$publicId.*" |~ "(?i)$search" [$__auto]))

panels.title: Affected Players (distinct publicId)
panels.description: Number of distinct publicIds with anomaly reports in the time window (anon = pre-login anomalies, merged into one).

targets.expr: count(sum by (publicId) (count_over_time({source="client", kind="anomaly"} | logfmt | type=~"$type" | platform=~"$platform" [$__range])))

panels.title: Anomaly Event Details (filter by type / platform / publicId / keyword)
panels.description: Each line ends with type=…publicId=…platform=…detail=…msg=… — the structured fields reported by the client. detail is supplemental info already packed into a single string on the client (heap/pool usage, FPS, stallMs, crash aliveMs+lastError, etc.).
